thm:1.1.summary: $\#\{p \in \mathcal{A} : p < X\} \asymp \#\mathcal{A} / \log X \asymp X^{\log 9 / \log 10} / \log X$, where $\mathcal{A}$ is integers $< X$ with no digit equal to $a_0$ in base 10. Confirms the Hardy-Littlewood prediction for a sparse digit-restricted set.

thm:1.2.summary: Generalization to base $q$: missing $s \le q^{23/80}$ digits in base $q$ gives $\#$ primes $\asymp X^{\log(q-s)/\log q} / \log X$. For special $\mathcal{B}$ (initial or final segment), $s \le q^{1/2-\varepsilon}$.

prop:6.1.summary: Sieve asymptotic terms: bilinear/multilinear sum over primes $p_1 \le \cdots \le p_\ell$ in dyadic ranges with restricted-digit indicator gives $o(\#\mathcal{A} / \log X)$ error in the asymptotic regime $\theta_1 = 9/25 + 2\varepsilon, \theta_2 = 17/40 - 2\varepsilon$.

prop:6.2.summary: Type II terms: bilinear sum control over the bilinear regime $[\theta_1, \theta_2]$. The hardest range, requires Fourier analysis on $\mathcal{A}$.

prop:7.1.summary: Type I estimate: $\mathcal{A}$ is equidistributed in residue classes $\bmod\,q$ for $q \le X^{50/77} (\log X)^{-2A-2}$. Beyond Bombieri-Vinogradov by exploiting the digit-restriction structure of $\mathcal{A}$.

prop:7.2.summary: Companion Type I estimate at intermediate ranges. Together with prop:7.1 supplies the equidistribution input for Buchstab-style sieve decomposition.

lem:7.3.summary: Fourier-analytic estimate on the indicator $\mathbf{1}_{\mathcal{A}}$: bounds on the $L^\infty$ norm of the Fourier transform of the digit-restricted set, yielding the equidistribution-in-AP results.

lem:7.4.summary: Refined Fourier bound for prop:7.1 / prop:7.2.

lem:8.1.summary: Combinatorial decomposition lemma in the spirit of Heath-Brown's identity, adapted to the bilinear regime needed by prop:6.2.

edges.note: Type I equidistribution of $\mathcal{A}$ in APs.
Heath-Brown-style combinatorial decomposition for the Type II bilinear range.

main_results.description: thm:1.1 (decimal, missing one digit), thm:1.2 (base $q$, missing up to $q^{23/80}$ digits).

sieve_decomposition.description: prop:6.1 (sieve asymptotic), prop:6.2 (Type II terms), lem:8.1 (Heath-Brown-style decomposition). The combinatorial backbone.

fourier_layer.description: prop:7.1, prop:7.2 (Type I equidistribution at level $X^{50/77}$); lem:7.3, lem:7.4 (Fourier-analytic bounds on $\mathbf{1}_{\mathcal{A}}$). The arithmetic structure of the digit-restricted set.

Maynard:smallgaps.title: Small gaps between primes
Maynard:smallgaps.role: method_source — same author's sieve framework, applied to a totally different problem

HeathBrown:Identity.title: Prime number theorem with error term (Heath-Brown identity)
HeathBrown:Identity.role: foundational — combinatorial decomposition used in lem:8.1

BombieriFriedlanderIwaniec:1986.title: Primes in arithmetic progressions to large moduli

Vinogradov:1937.title: Vinogradov's method for prime number sums
Vinogradov:1937.role: foundational — Type I/II decomposition originates here
